answers.label: Creating technology — writing code, building apps, or working with AI
Deep solo work — headphones on, shipping features and fixing bugs
Creating — designing interfaces, building new features from scratch

milestones.title: Certification
milestones.description: (Optional) Complete an online certificate, bootcamp, or structured course in software developer to build a strong learning foundation

milestones.title: Core Skill Learning
milestones.description: Learn the fundamentals: build a solid foundation in programming (python, java, or c++), data structures and algorithms, and version control (git) — these are the essential building blocks for a software developer

resources.title: Beginner C++ Game Development Tutorials on Udemy
Introductory Data Structures and Algorithms Courses on Coursera
freeCodeCamp Git Version Control for Beginners Crash Course on YouTube
freeCodeCamp Python Programming Fundamentals for Beginners on YouTube

milestones.title: Intermediate / Advanced Skills
milestones.description: Deepen your expertise by developing intermediate-to-advanced skills in restful api development, database management (sql), and debugging and troubleshooting, applying them through exercises and case studies

milestones.title: Hands-on Practice
milestones.description: Gain hands-on experience by completing software projects, open-source contributions, or technical prototypes — focus on applying programming (python, java, or c++), and data structures and algorithms in realistic scenarios

milestones.title: Portfolio Building
milestones.description: Build a professional portfolio: publish your best projects on GitHub, create a personal website showcasing your work, and document your process and results

milestones.title: CV / Profile Building
milestones.description: Prepare your professional profile: craft a targeted CV/resume highlighting your software developer skills, optimize your LinkedIn profile, and gather references or endorsements from mentors, instructors, or past collaborators
